basics.name: Md Rezwanul Haque
basics.label: MASc Candidate · AI Researcher
basics.summary: MASc candidate in Electrical & Computer Engineering at the University of Waterloo, specializing in Multimodal Machine Learning (MMML) and Large Language Models (LLMs) under the supervision of Prof. Fakhri Karray at the CPAMI Lab. Published at top-tier venues including ICCV, IEEE SMC, and IJCNN. Secured 2nd place in the ICCV MSLR-2025 Challenge with a Best Poster Award. Research interests span multimodal AI agents, human-centered machine intelligence, and sign language recognition.

work.name: Centre for Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (CPAMI) Lab
work.summary: Conducting research on multimodal machine learning and large language models for human-centered AI applications, including depression detection, sign language recognition, and medical image analysis.

courses: Research Focus: Multimodal Machine Learning, Large Language Models, and Human-Centered AI.
Thesis: Multimodal Machine Learning and Large Language Models for Human-Centered Applications.
Faculty of Engineering (FOE) Award recipient (Winter & Spring 2025).
Thesis: “A Study on Non-Invasive Hemoglobin Measurement Techniques and Predictions.”
